name: Order Condition for Linear Multistep Methods
statement_text:
A linear multistep formula [α, β] has order p if and only if

                       z      α(1 + z)
                                       + β(1 + z) = O(z p ).
                   log(1 + z)    z

variables.name: α
variables.definition: α is the polynomial associated with the linear multistep method, typically α(z) = α_k z^k + α_{k-1} z^{k-1} + ... + α_0

variables.name: β
variables.definition: β is the polynomial associated with the linear multistep method, typically β(z) = β_k z^k + β_{k-1} z^{k-1} + ... + β_0

variables.name: p
variables.definition: p is the order of the linear multistep method

variables.name: log(1+z)
variables.definition: log(1+z) is defined for |z|<1 by its power series: log(1+z) = z - (1/2)z^2 + (1/3)z^3 - ...

preamble: The theorem concerns the order condition for a linear multistep method [α, β]. The preceding text derives an alternative form of the order condition by substituting exp(z) with (1+z)^{-1} and using the power series for log(1+z).

dependencies.name: Order Condition for Linear Multistep Methods
